images.caption:
Figure 1:A visual tool call can help or hurt.Example 1:a crop turns a
wrong answer right (useful);Example 2:a crop flips a
would-be-correct answer to wrong (misleading).

Figure 2:Overview ofTACO.(a)The accuracy channelA1A_{1}scores the
final answer; the process channelA2A_{2}is the before/after probe difference (the
tool-call value), gated into the loss only whenΔ≥0\Delta{\geq}0.(b)OGARsendsA1A_{1}to a segment only when it is responsible for the answer (solid box),
gating it out otherwise (dashed box).

Appendix BTraining Algorithm and Edge Cases

images.caption:
Figure 4:Reading an occluded sponsor wordmark: the agent crops the backdrop banner and
zooms2×2\timesto recover “BNP Paribas”.

Figure 5:High-resolution perception: the agent crops and zooms a circuit board to read the
three small labels printed above a connector.

Figure 6:Scientific-figure reading: the agent isolates one panel of a six-method
precision plot to extract the requested value.

Figure 7:Image rotation: the photo was uploaded sideways, so the agent rotates it before
reading the route number on the bus’s destination board.

Figure 8:Chart-grounded math: the agent crops the relevant chart region and computes the
requested fraction.
